instructions: Score each dimension yes/no/partial against a concrete loop. A good loop scores yes across the board. Map low scores to the linked node for the fix.

dimensions.question: Is the goal single and clear?
Is acceptance machine-verifiable (a script returns pass/fail)?
Does the agent get sufficient context (code, conventions, constraints)?
Are steps small enough that each is checkable?
Is there per-step feedback, not just an end check?
On failure does it change strategy rather than head-bang?
Are there max-iterations and a token/cost budget?
Are there permission boundaries (least privilege, sandbox)?
Does it produce an auditable final summary (root cause, files, checks, results, residual risk)?
Is the checker separate from the maker?
Is state externalized so it survives context resets?

verdict_rule: All dimensions yes → industrial loop. Any of {machine_acceptance, iteration_budget_cap, checker_separate, permission_boundaries} no → do not run unattended.
